title: The death and rebirth of my home server
content_html:
A few evenings ago my partner and I were in the mood to install this particular Linux ISO. The guys from Red Letter Systems had spoken of it well, it had good reviews on boundingboxd, and my partner liked the installation trailer.

I opened Tremotesf on my phone to check whether I already had it on the Pi, but the torrent list wouldn't load. The SMB mount wouldn't mount. I couldn't SSH into it either.

Not eager to ruin the evening by starting a debugging session then and there, I chalked it up to a simple issue with the recent changes I made to the NixOS configuration and resolved to take a look at it the next day.

The next day

I hooked the Pi up to the dedicated debugging display I have in the closet, cracked knuckles, said "it's debugging time" or something to that effect, and rebooted the brick.

The current NixOS generation started booting, loaded the kernel and... hung there. No more output. The previous generation did the same. I took the microSD card out for further inspection.

The first obvious step was to fsck it; the power had mysteriously gone out in the whole building a few days prior, and perhaps it happened right when one of the blocks containing some files important for the boot process was being written? Anyway, it was worth a check. fsck.ext4 reported and fixed lots of errors. Running it again reported the FS as clean, but ejecting and then plugging the card back in turned up more errors...

Long story short, the card was likely dead, and the blackout had simply delivered the coup de grace. Press F to pay respects.

Taking inventory

This Raspberry Pi 4B had been running for years almost 24/7 so this didn't come as a surprise. My understanding is that SD cards can sustain a comparatively small number of write cycles before they start to fail. The surprising part was that mine managed to run for so long with no issues, considering how badly I'd been treating it. The microSD was mounted as the root FS and I had taken next to no precautions to minimize writes.

I didn't lose much, all things considered. The important stuff mostly lived in external HDDs and the configuration lived almost entirely in my NixOS configuration. The only things that lived on the microSD card and I had no backup for were .torrent files and caches for Navidrome, Jellyfin, and slskd, all of which I could easily regenerate. Immich's data was safely backed up on an external drive and I was taking regular backups, so nothing was lost there.

I've never had a "primary" drive die on me like this before; the worst I've seen were a few dead flash drives that contained nothing important. The fear of a dead drive and lost data has resided in the back of my mind ever since I was a teenager and accidentally the main OS X partition on my macbook while trying to install Linux for the first time, but now it's worse than ever, so I wanted to take strong precautions.

Configuring the new setup

I set out to rebuild my home server with a few things in mind:

  • Try to minimize writes to the microSD.
  • Have some redundancy in the data stored on external hard drives.
  • Back up everything important.

Swap, /tmp, and other volatile data

Swap is useful to have, even with the 8GB of RAM on my Pi, but I don't think swapping to the microSD is a good idea if I want it to live long, so I could either swap to an external HDD (very slow) or use zram.

zram, formerly called compcache, is a Linux kernel module for creating a compressed block device in RAM, i.e. a RAM disk with on-the-fly disk compression.

The common uses for zram are swap or /tmp. I chose to enable zram for swap but not for /tmp, which I kept as a normal RAM disk; I'm not 100% sure how these two features interact but I figured I'd let the kernel figure out how and when to swap out the cold pages in /tmp to the compressed swap rather than reserving a separate block for it.

{
  # Enable in-memory compressed swap device
  zramSwap.enable = true;

  # Use a RAM disk for /tmp
  boot.tmp.useTmpfs = true;
}

I also chose to have journald log to memory rather than to disk. I need to investigate whether it's possible to log to memory and only occasionally flush to disk (in which case I'd mount /var/log to a subvolume on an HDD) but I haven't figured that part out yet.

{
  # Store journal logs in memory to /run/log/journal
  services.journald.storage = "volatile";
}

The microSD card remains mounted to /, but I disabled atime because it causes writes whenever a file is accessed and I don't think it's very useful.

{
  fileSystems."/" = {
    device = "/dev/disk/by-label/takodachi";
    fsType = "ext4";
    options = [ "noatime" ];
  };
}

External HDDs

If you live in the current year of 2026 you probably know this is not a good time to be buying any new drives (or RAM, or GPUs, or anything that can be used to build datacenters and feed the pockets of executives enraptured by AI psychosis) and that it's a better idea to get scrappy and creative with what you have.

I have tons of drives lying around of all sorts of makes, sizes, and ages. I treat them mostly as cold storage for old data that I kinda want to keep; they're the digital equivalent of a junk drawer. I've always wanted to immolate them for a higher purpose but never quite found one until now. I took two of these old 2.5" HDDs from the pile.

The first was a 500GB drive that contained a Windows filesystem. This used in my partner's laptop; a 3KG behemoth from another era of business machines, meant more as a desktop you could easily move to another desk and occasionally bring home rather than a truly portable machine. First I swapped out the HDD with an SSD as an act of mercy on the dying laptop; eventually I set my partner up with a Surface Pro 5 running NixOS and they kindly gave me the old laptop and HDD to play around with.

The second was a 320GB drive that seemed to contain... a home assistant installation? I have no idea how that ended up there. Anyway, this was a Hitachi drive with an apple logo on it, so it used to be inside either my 2006 white plastic macbook or a mac mini of a similar vintage.

I connected these two pieces of history to a docking station and created a btrfs pool with raid1 replication. I fittingly named it ポンコツ (piece of junk).

sudo mkfs.btrfs --data raid1 --metadata raid1 --label ponkotsu /dev/sdX /dev/sdY

I went with btrfs because it's what I know (it runs on my desktop and laptop), I like subvolumes (more on that later), and I like the flexibility of its pools. You don't have to plan the topology of the pool beforehand, you can add drives to the pool whenever you want, and they don't have to be of the same size.

Subvolumes

My plan was to have one btrfs subvolume per service, and to do it declaratively I created an autosubvol module. The configuration looks like this:

{
  services.autosubvol = {
    enable = true;
    disks.ponkotsu = {
      device = "/dev/disk/by-uuid/<pool uuid>";
      subvolumes.immich = {
        mountPoint = "/var/lib/immich";
        mountOptions = [ "noatime" ];
        requiredBy = [ "immich-server.service" ];
      };
    };
  };
}

The module creates some systemd units:

  • One .mount unit for each disk at /run/btrfs-roots/<name>.
  • One autosubvol-ensure-<disk>-<subvolume>.service oneshot unit with a script that checks whether the subvolume exists and, if not, creates it. It's configured with RemainAfterExit=true so that the .mount unit for the subvolume can depend on it.
  • One .mount unit for each subvolume, mounted to the specified mountPoint. It depends on the autosubvol-ensure .service and sets Before= and RequiredBy= on the units it is requiredBy.

I think this makes each service module look very clean.

{
  config,
  ...
}:
{
  services.immich = {
    enable = true;
    # ...
  };

  services.autosubvol.disks.ponkotsu.subvolumes.immich = {
    mountPoint = config.services.immich.mediaLocation;
    mountOptions = [ "noatime" ];
    requiredBy = [ "immich-server.service" ];
  };
}

I also created a subvolume for /var/cache for good measure.

{
  fileSystems."/var/cache" = {
    device = "/dev/disk/by-uuid/...";
    fsType = "btrfs";
    options = [
      "subvol=@cache"
      "noatime"
      "nofail"
    ];
  };
}

Backups

Back when I set up Immich I also created a Nix module that acts as a bridge between sops-nix and the restic nixpkgs module. The implementation is only a few lines of code, and it looks like this in my Nix configuration:

{
  internal.backups.restic.immich = {
    paths = [ "/var/lib/immich" ];
    exclude = [
      "/var/lib/immich/encoded-video"
      "/var/lib/immich/thumbs"
    ];
    pruneOpts = [
      "--keep-daily 7"
      "--keep-weekly 4"
      "--keep-monthly 3"
    ];
  };
}

Since each service is on its own subvolume I could probably set up a local backup system using snapshots and btrfs send with a disk entirely dedicated to backups that I'd keep powered off most of the time, but since right now I don't have that many disks and it's getting increasingly difficult to get new ones, I'm gonna leave that for better times. An S3 bucket and replication will have to do.

Groups and permissions

I want several services to have access to my Linux ISOs: Transmission, Jellyfin, Navidrome, and a few others. I handled this by creating a media group and assigning it as an extra group to all the applications that need to read or write those files.

{
  users.groups.media = {};
  users.users.steenuil.extraGroups = [ "media" ];
  users.users.jellyfin.extraGroups = [ "media" ];

  services.transmission = {
    group = "media";
    downloadDirPermissions = "2775";
    settings.umask = "002";
  };
}

The part that makes this work is Transmission's downloadDirPermissions set to 2775. The 2 indicates that the setgid bit is on, which means that if /srv/linux-isos is owned by the media group, every file and directory created inside it will inherit the media group rather than the creator's primary group.

Monitoring

This is one part I haven't fully figured out yet. The only precaution I have taken is to regularly run btrfs scrubs on the pool to connect any errors and decide whether to take a closer look at the S.M.A.R.T stats based on the number of errors on each drive.

I'd like to set up smartd and perhaps connect it to a nice dashboard like Scrutiny, but since I'm running many other services and I wouldn't mind having more statistics on them, perhaps it'd be more useful to set up Prometheus and then figure out how to display everything on a nice Grafana dashboard? I know next to nothing about Grafana so it all looks a bit daunting and overkill to me, but it might be a good learning experience.

Feeding the pool

Let's get back to the spinning rust; we were at two disks in the pool. I wanted to add a 1TB drive to the pool while also copying its contents to it. The disk was already partitioned exactly in half and most of the data was in its back half, so I formatted the front half as btrfs, added it to the pool, copied the back half to the pool, and then extended the btrfs partition to fill the disk. I'm not sure how effective this was in terms of speed, but it saved me a btrfs balance.

To save the Pi some trouble I set up Immich on my desktop and restored from the backup there, and since I avoided moving the thumbnails in the previous steps I regenerated all of them in webp.

This is the current state of the pool: a 1TB disk, a 500GB, and a 320GB one; raid1 for both data and metadata. I'm planning to add another old 500GB disk to it and I'll probably keep it that way until either drive prices go down, or one of the drives dies.

I also have a 10TB disk still formatted with ext4 and filled to the brim with Linux ISOs that I can't reasonably add to the pool, so I'll just back up whatever I think is important and ride it until it dies. #yolo

The new microSD

Since I'm putting much less pressure on the microSD card I went with a 64GB one rather than the 128GB I used before. I searched for a "high endurance" card because they're supposed to have more write cycles before they give up the ghost.

I'm not completely sure what I got in the end though. This whole ordeal happened just a few days prior to the vacation I'm writing this post from, so I ordered a card that would arrive on Saturday: the day before I left. Saturday morning came and the package still hadn't shipped, so I had to cancel it, get off my ass, and walk to the nearest home appliance store.

It wasn't too bad. I took a nice stroll, took a photo of some of those Asian beetles that are invading southern Europe and leaving a trail of half eaten leaves in their wake.

The store had AC. I had a look at the latest gimmick phones. I walked out with a 64GB microSD card claiming to be high endurance, and even a discounted UPS.

Deployment

NixOS is an intimidating project when you're starting out. There's way too much information scattered through the interwebs to fully grasp it. You get used to it though. Your brain does the translating.

You could bootstrap a NixOS installation on a Pi by downloading the official NixOS image and start from an empty system, or you could just know in your heart of hearts that all you need is this module:

{
  modulesPath,
  ...
}:
{
  imports = [
    "${modulesPath}/installer/sd-card/sd-image-aarch64.nix"
  ];

  sdImage.rootVolumeLabel = "takodachi";
}

To be able to cast this incantation:

nix build .#nixosConfigurations.takodachi.config.system.build.sdImage

Which will build an .img file that you can dd to a microSD card to get your system up and running, with no intermediary steps.

Finishing up

After imaging the microSD card all I had to do was hook everything up to the new UPS and plug it in, and in a few seconds I could SSH into the Pi and start resolving some minor issues with the new configuration.

I left for my vacation a bit late, but with Navidrome up and running and able to serve music to listen to on the drive there.

sentences.en: The previous generation did the same.
sentences.zh: 上一代也做了同样的事情。

sentences.en: I took the microSD card out for further inspection.
sentences.zh: 我把microSD卡拿出来进一步检查。

blocks.html_en: The first obvious step was to fsck it; the power had mysteriously gone out in the whole building a few days prior, and perhaps it happened right when one of the blocks containing some files important for the boot process was being written? Anyway, it was worth a check. fsck.ext4 reported and fixed lots of errors. Running it again reported the FS as clean, but ejecting and then plugging the card back in turned up more errors...

sentences.en: The first obvious step was to fsck it; the power had mysteriously gone out in the whole building a few days prior, and perhaps it happened right when one of the blocks containing some files important for the boot process was being written?
sentences.zh: 第一个明显的步骤是对其进行 fsck；几天前，整栋大楼神秘地断电了，也许就在写入包含一些对启动过程很重要的文件的块之一时？

sentences.en: Anyway, it was worth a check.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，这是值得一试的。

sentences.en: fsck.ext4 reported and fixed lots of errors.
sentences.zh: fsck.ext4 报告并修复了许多错误。

sentences.en: Running it again reported the FS as clean, but ejecting and then plugging the card back in turned up more errors...
sentences.zh: 再次运行它报告 FS 是干净的，但是弹出然后将卡重新插入会出现更多错误......

blocks.html_en: Long story short, the card was likely dead, and the blackout had simply delivered the coup de grace. Press F to pay respects.

sentences.en: Long story short, the card was likely dead, and the blackout had simply delivered the coup de grace.
sentences.zh: 长话短说，这张卡很可能已经死了，而停电只是造成了最后的致命一击。

sentences.en: Press F to pay respects.
sentences.zh: 按F表示敬意。

blocks.html_en: Taking inventory

sentences.en: Taking inventory
sentences.zh: 盘点库存

blocks.html_en: This Raspberry Pi 4B had been running for years almost 24/7 so this didn't come as a surprise. My understanding is that SD cards can sustain a comparatively small number of write cycles before they start to fail. The surprising part was that mine managed to run for so long with no issues, considering how badly I'd been treating it. The microSD was mounted as the root FS and I had taken next to no precautions to minimize writes.

sentences.en: This Raspberry Pi 4B had been running for years almost 24/7 so this didn't come as a surprise.
sentences.zh: 这个 Raspberry Pi 4B 已经运行了很多年，几乎 24/7，所以这并不令人意外。

sentences.en: My understanding is that SD cards can sustain a comparatively small number of write cycles before they start to fail.
sentences.zh: 我的理解是，SD 卡在出现故障之前可以承受相对较少的写入周期。

sentences.en: The surprising part was that mine managed to run for so long with no issues, considering how badly I'd been treating it.
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，考虑到我对待它的态度有多糟糕，我的机器竟然运行了这么长时间，没有出现任何问题。

sentences.en: The microSD was mounted as the root FS and I had taken next to no precautions to minimize writes.
sentences.zh: microSD 作为根 FS 挂载，我几乎没有采取任何预防措施来最大程度地减少写入。

blocks.html_en: I didn't lose much, all things considered. The important stuff mostly lived in external HDDs and the configuration lived almost entirely in my NixOS configuration. The only things that lived on the microSD card and I had no backup for were .torrent files and caches for Navidrome, Jellyfin, and slskd, all of which I could easily regenerate. Immich's data was safely backed up on an external drive and I was taking regular backups, so nothing was lost there.

sentences.en: I didn't lose much, all things considered.
sentences.zh: 综合考虑，我并没有损失太多。

sentences.en: The important stuff mostly lived in external HDDs and the configuration lived almost entirely in my NixOS configuration.
sentences.zh: 重要的东西大多存在于外部 HDD 中，而配置几乎完全存在于我的 NixOS 配置中。

sentences.en: The only things that lived on the microSD card and I had no backup for were .torrent files and caches for Navidrome, Jellyfin, and slskd, all of which I could easily regenerate.
sentences.zh: microSD 卡上唯一存在且我没有备份的东西是 .torrent 文件以及 Navidrome、Jellyfin 和 slskd 的缓存，所有这些我都可以轻松地重新生成。

sentences.en: Immich's data was safely backed up on an external drive and I was taking regular backups, so nothing was lost there.
sentences.zh: Immich 的数据安全地备份在外部驱动器上，并且我定期进行备份，因此没有丢失任何内容。

blocks.html_en: I've never had a "primary" drive die on me like this before; the worst I've seen were a few dead flash drives that contained nothing important. The fear of a dead drive and lost data has resided in the back of my mind ever since I was a teenager and accidentally the main OS X partition on my macbook while trying to install Linux for the first time, but now it's worse than ever, so I wanted to take strong precautions.

sentences.en: I've never had a "primary" drive die on me like this before; the worst I've seen were a few dead flash drives that contained nothing important.
sentences.zh: 我以前从来没有遇到过像这样的“主要”驱动器消失的情况；我见过的最糟糕的是一些失效的闪存驱动器，其中没有包含任何重要的内容。

sentences.en: The fear of a dead drive and lost data has resided in the back of my mind ever since I was a teenager and accidentally the main OS X partition on my macbook while trying to install Linux for the first time, but now it's worse than ever, so I wanted to take strong precautions.
sentences.zh: 自从我十几岁的时候第一次尝试安装 Linux 时不小心损坏了我的 MacBook 上的主 OS X 分区以来，对驱动器失效和数据丢失的恐惧就一直存在于我的脑海中，但现在情况比以往任何时候都更糟，所以我想采取强有力的预防措施。

blocks.html_en: Configuring the new setup

sentences.en: Configuring the new setup
sentences.zh: 配置新设置

blocks.html_en: I set out to rebuild my home server with a few things in mind:

sentences.en: I set out to rebuild my home server with a few things in mind:
sentences.zh: 我开始重建我的家庭服务器，考虑到以下几点：

blocks.html_en: • Try to minimize writes to the microSD.

sentences.en: Try to minimize writes to the microSD.
sentences.zh: 尽量减少对 microSD 的写入。

blocks.html_en: • Have some redundancy in the data stored on external hard drives.

sentences.en: Have some redundancy in the data stored on external hard drives.
sentences.zh: 外部硬盘驱动器上存储的数据有一些冗余。

blocks.html_en: • Back up everything important.

sentences.en: Back up everything important.
sentences.zh: 备份所有重要的东西。

blocks.html_en: Swap, /tmp, and other volatile data

sentences.en: Swap, /tmp, and other volatile data
sentences.zh: Swap、/tmp 和其他易失性数据

blocks.html_en: Swap is useful to have, even with the 8GB of RAM on my Pi, but I don't think swapping to the microSD is a good idea if I want it to live long, so I could either swap to an external HDD (very slow) or use zram.

sentences.en: Swap is useful to have, even with the 8GB of RAM on my Pi, but I don't think swapping to the microSD is a good idea if I want it to live long, so I could either swap to an external HDD (very slow) or use zram.
sentences.zh: 即使我的 Pi 上有 8GB RAM，交换功能也很有用，但如果我希望它能长久使用，我认为交换到 microSD 并不是一个好主意，所以我可以交换到外部 HDD（非常慢）或使用 zram。

blocks.html_en: zram, formerly called compcache, is a Linux kernel module for creating a compressed block device in RAM, i.e. a RAM disk with on-the-fly disk compression.

sentences.en: zram, formerly called compcache, is a Linux kernel module for creating a compressed block device in RAM, i.e. a RAM disk with on-the-fly disk compression.
sentences.zh: zram，以前称为 compcache，是一个 Linux 内核模块，用于在 RAM 中创建压缩块设备，即具有即时磁盘压缩功能的 RAM 磁盘。

blocks.html_en: zram, formerly called compcache, is a Linux kernel module for creating a compressed block device in RAM, i.e. a RAM disk with on-the-fly disk compression.

sentences.en: zram, formerly called compcache, is a Linux kernel module for creating a compressed block device in RAM, i.e. a RAM disk with on-the-fly disk compression.
sentences.zh: zram，以前称为 compcache，是一个 Linux 内核模块，用于在 RAM 中创建压缩块设备，即具有即时磁盘压缩功能的 RAM 磁盘。

blocks.html_en: The common uses for zram are swap or /tmp. I chose to enable zram for swap but not for /tmp, which I kept as a normal RAM disk; I'm not 100% sure how these two features interact but I figured I'd let the kernel figure out how and when to swap out the cold pages in /tmp to the compressed swap rather than reserving a separate block for it.

sentences.en: The common uses for zram are swap or /tmp.
sentences.zh: zram 的常见用途是 swap 或 /tmp。

sentences.en: I chose to enable zram for swap but not for /tmp, which I kept as a normal RAM disk; I'm not 100% sure how these two features interact but I figured I'd let the kernel figure out how and when to swap out the cold pages in /tmp to the compressed swap rather than reserving a separate block for it.
sentences.zh: 我选择为交换启用 zram，但不为 /tmp 启用，我将其保留为普通 RAM 磁盘；我不是 100% 确定这两个功能如何交互，但我想我应该让内核弄清楚如何以及何时将 /tmp 中的冷页交换到压缩交换，而不是为其保留单独的块。

blocks.html_en: I also chose to have journald log to memory rather than to disk. I need to investigate whether it's possible to log to memory and only occasionally flush to disk (in which case I'd mount /var/log to a subvolume on an HDD) but I haven't figured that part out yet.

sentences.en: I also chose to have journald log to memory rather than to disk.
sentences.zh: 我还选择将日志记录到内存而不是磁盘。

sentences.en: I need to investigate whether it's possible to log to memory and only occasionally flush to disk (in which case I'd mount /var/log to a subvolume on an HDD) but I haven't figured that part out yet.
sentences.zh: 我需要调查是否可以记录到内存，并且只是偶尔刷新到磁盘（在这种情况下，我会将 /var/log 安装到 HDD 上的子卷），但我还没有弄清楚这部分。

blocks.html_en: The microSD card remains mounted to /, but I disabled atime because it causes writes whenever a file is accessed and I don't think it's very useful.

sentences.en: The microSD card remains mounted to /, but I disabled atime because it causes writes whenever a file is accessed and I don't think it's very useful.
sentences.zh: microSD 卡仍然安装在 / 上，但我禁用了 atime，因为每当访问文件时它都会导致写入，我认为它不是很有用。

blocks.html_en: External HDDs

sentences.en: External HDDs
sentences.zh: 外置硬盘

blocks.html_en: If you live in the current year of 2026 you probably know this is not a good time to be buying any new drives (or RAM, or GPUs, or anything that can be used to build datacenters and feed the pockets of executives enraptured by AI psychosis) and that it's a better idea to get scrappy and creative with what you have.

sentences.en: If you live in the current year of 2026 you probably know this is not a good time to be buying any new drives (or RAM, or GPUs, or anything that can be used to build datacenters and feed the pockets of executives enraptured by AI psychosis) and that it's a better idea to get scrappy and creative with what you have.
sentences.zh: 如果您生活在 2026 年，您可能知道现在不是购买任何新驱动器（或 RAM、GPU，或任何可用于构建数据中心并满足痴迷于 AI 精神病的高管的口袋的东西）的好时机，最好利用您所拥有的东西来发挥斗志和创造力。

blocks.html_en: I have tons of drives lying around of all sorts of makes, sizes, and ages. I treat them mostly as cold storage for old data that I kinda want to keep; they're the digital equivalent of a junk drawer. I've always wanted to immolate them for a higher purpose but never quite found one until now. I took two of these old 2.5" HDDs from the pile.

sentences.en: I have tons of drives lying around of all sorts of makes, sizes, and ages.
sentences.zh: 我有大量各种品牌、尺寸和寿命的驱动器。

sentences.en: I treat them mostly as cold storage for old data that I kinda want to keep; they're the digital equivalent of a junk drawer.
sentences.zh: 我主要将它们视为我想保留的旧数据的冷存储；它们相当于数字垃圾抽屉。

sentences.en: I've always wanted to immolate them for a higher purpose but never quite found one until now.
sentences.zh: 我一直想为了更高的目的而牺牲它们，但直到现在才找到一个。

sentences.en: I took two of these old 2.5" HDDs from the pile.
sentences.zh: 我从一堆旧的 2.5 英寸硬盘中取出了两个。

blocks.html_en: The first was a 500GB drive that contained a Windows filesystem. This used in my partner's laptop; a 3KG behemoth from another era of business machines, meant more as a desktop you could easily move to another desk and occasionally bring home rather than a truly portable machine. First I swapped out the HDD with an SSD as an act of mercy on the dying laptop; eventually I set my partner up with a Surface Pro 5 running NixOS and they kindly gave me the old laptop and HDD to play around with.

sentences.en: The first was a 500GB drive that contained a Windows filesystem.
sentences.zh: 第一个是包含 Windows 文件系统的 500GB 驱动器。

sentences.en: This used in my partner's laptop; a 3KG behemoth from another era of business machines, meant more as a desktop you could easily move to another desk and occasionally bring home rather than a truly portable machine.
sentences.zh: 这用在我伙伴的笔记本电脑上；来自另一个商务机器时代的 3KG 庞然大物，更多地意味着它是一台可以轻松移动到另一张桌子并偶尔带回家的台式机，而不是真正的便携式机器。

sentences.en: First I swapped out the HDD with an SSD as an act of mercy on the dying laptop; eventually I set my partner up with a Surface Pro 5 running NixOS and they kindly gave me the old laptop and HDD to play around with.
sentences.zh: 首先，我将 HDD 换成了 SSD，作为对即将死去的笔记本电脑的怜悯之举；最终，我为我的合作伙伴安装了一台运行 NixOS 的 Surface Pro 5，他们好心地给了我旧笔记本电脑和硬盘来使用。

blocks.html_en: The second was a 320GB drive that seemed to contain... a home assistant installation? I have no idea how that ended up there. Anyway, this was a Hitachi drive with an apple logo on it, so it used to be inside either my 2006 white plastic macbook or a mac mini of a similar vintage.

sentences.en: The second was a 320GB drive that seemed to contain...
sentences.zh: 第二个是 320GB 驱动器，似乎包含...

sentences.en: a home assistant installation?
sentences.zh: 家庭助理安装？

sentences.en: I have no idea how that ended up there.
sentences.zh: 我不知道那是怎么结束的。

sentences.en: Anyway, this was a Hitachi drive with an apple logo on it, so it used to be inside either my 2006 white plastic macbook or a mac mini of a similar vintage.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，这是一个带有苹果标志的日立硬盘，所以它曾经放在我的 2006 年白色塑料 MacBook 或类似年份的 mac mini 中。

blocks.html_en: I connected these two pieces of history to a docking station and created a btrfs pool with raid1 replication. I fittingly named it ポンコツ (piece of junk).

sentences.en: I connected these two pieces of history to a docking station and created a btrfs pool with raid1 replication.
sentences.zh: 我将这两条历史记录连接到一个扩展坞，并创建了一个具有 raid1 复制的 btrfs 池。

sentences.en: I fittingly named it ポンコツ (piece of junk).
sentences.zh: 我恰当地将它命名为“垃圾”。

blocks.html_en: I went with btrfs because it's what I know (it runs on my desktop and laptop), I like subvolumes (more on that later), and I like the flexibility of its pools. You don't have to plan the topology of the pool beforehand, you can add drives to the pool whenever you want, and they don't have to be of the same size.

sentences.en: I went with btrfs because it's what I know (it runs on my desktop and laptop), I like subvolumes (more on that later), and I like the flexibility of its pools.
sentences.zh: 我选择了 btrfs，因为这是我所知道的（它在我的台式机和笔记本电脑上运行），我喜欢子卷（稍后会详细介绍），而且我喜欢它的池的灵活性。

sentences.en: You don't have to plan the topology of the pool beforehand, you can add drives to the pool whenever you want, and they don't have to be of the same size.
sentences.zh: 您不必事先规划池的拓扑，可以随时将驱动器添加到池中，并且它们的大小不必相同。

blocks.html_en: Subvolumes

sentences.en: Subvolumes
sentences.zh: 子卷

blocks.html_en: My plan was to have one btrfs subvolume per service, and to do it declaratively I created an autosubvol module. The configuration looks like this:

sentences.en: My plan was to have one btrfs subvolume per service, and to do it declaratively I created an autosubvol module.
sentences.zh: 我的计划是每个服务都有一个 btrfs 子卷，并且为了以声明方式执行此操作，我创建了一个 autosubvol 模块。

sentences.en: The configuration looks like this:
sentences.zh: 配置如下：

blocks.html_en: The module creates some systemd units:

sentences.en: The module creates some systemd units:
sentences.zh: 该模块创建一些 systemd 单元：

blocks.html_en: • One .mount unit for each disk at /run/btrfs-roots/<name>.

sentences.en: One .mount unit for each disk at /run/btrfs-roots/<name>.
sentences.zh: /run/btrfs-roots/<name> 处的每个磁盘有一个 .mount 单元。

blocks.html_en: • One autosubvol-ensure-<disk>-<subvolume>.service oneshot unit with a script that checks whether the subvolume exists and, if not, creates it. It's configured with RemainAfterExit=true so that the .mount unit for the subvolume can depend on it.

sentences.en: One autosubvol-ensure-<disk>-<subvolume>.service oneshot unit with a script that checks whether the subvolume exists and, if not, creates it.
sentences.zh: 一个 autosubvol-ensure-<disk>-<subvolume>.service oneshot 单元，其中包含一个脚本，用于检查子卷是否存在，如果不存在，则创建它。

sentences.en: It's configured with RemainAfterExit=true so that the .mount unit for the subvolume can depend on it.
sentences.zh: 它配置为 RemainAfterExit=true，以便子卷的 .mount 单元可以依赖它。

blocks.html_en: • One .mount unit for each subvolume, mounted to the specified mountPoint. It depends on the autosubvol-ensure .service and sets Before= and RequiredBy= on the units it is requiredBy.

sentences.en: One .mount unit for each subvolume, mounted to the specified mountPoint.
sentences.zh: 每个子卷一个 .mount 单元，安装到指定的安装点。

sentences.en: It depends on the autosubvol-ensure .service and sets Before= and RequiredBy= on the units it is requiredBy.
sentences.zh: 它取决于 autosubvol-ensure .service 并根据 requiredBy 的单位设置 Before= 和RequiredBy=。

blocks.html_en: I think this makes each service module look very clean.

sentences.en: I think this makes each service module look very clean.
sentences.zh: 我认为这使得每个服务模块看起来都很干净。

blocks.html_en: I also created a subvolume for /var/cache for good measure.

sentences.en: I also created a subvolume for /var/cache for good measure.
sentences.zh: 为了更好地衡量，我还为 /var/cache 创建了一个子卷。

blocks.html_en: Backups

sentences.en: Backups
sentences.zh: 备份

blocks.html_en: Back when I set up Immich I also created a Nix module that acts as a bridge between sops-nix and the restic nixpkgs module. The implementation is only a few lines of code, and it looks like this in my Nix configuration:

sentences.en: Back when I set up Immich I also created a Nix module that acts as a bridge between sops-nix and the restic nixpkgs module.
sentences.zh: 当我设置 Immich 时，我还创建了一个 Nix 模块，充当 sops-nix 和 Restic nixpkgs 模块之间的桥梁。

sentences.en: The implementation is only a few lines of code, and it looks like this in my Nix configuration:
sentences.zh: 实现只有几行代码，在我的 Nix 配置中如下所示：

blocks.html_en: Since each service is on its own subvolume I could probably set up a local backup system using snapshots and btrfs send with a disk entirely dedicated to backups that I'd keep powered off most of the time, but since right now I don't have that many disks and it's getting increasingly difficult to get new ones, I'm gonna leave that for better times. An S3 bucket and replication will have to do.

sentences.en: Since each service is on its own subvolume I could probably set up a local backup system using snapshots and btrfs send with a disk entirely dedicated to backups that I'd keep powered off most of the time, but since right now I don't have that many disks and it's getting increasingly difficult to get new ones, I'm gonna leave that for better times.
sentences.zh: 由于每个服务都位于其自己的子卷上，我可能可以使用快照和 btrfs 发送一个完全专用于备份的磁盘来设置本地备份系统，我大部分时间都会保持关闭状态，但由于现在我没有那么多磁盘，而且获取新磁盘变得越来越困难，所以我将把它留到更好的时候。

sentences.en: An S3 bucket and replication will have to do.
sentences.zh: 必须使用 S3 存储桶和复制。

blocks.html_en: Groups and permissions

sentences.en: Groups and permissions
sentences.zh: 组和权限

blocks.html_en: I want several services to have access to my Linux ISOs: Transmission, Jellyfin, Navidrome, and a few others. I handled this by creating a media group and assigning it as an extra group to all the applications that need to read or write those files.

sentences.en: I want several services to have access to my Linux ISOs: Transmission, Jellyfin, Navidrome, and a few others.
sentences.zh: 我希望多个服务能够访问我的 Linux ISO：Transmission、Jellyfin、Navidrome 以及其他一些服务。

sentences.en: I handled this by creating a media group and assigning it as an extra group to all the applications that need to read or write those files.
sentences.zh: 我通过创建一个媒体组并将其作为额外组分配给所有需要读取或写入这些文件的应用程序来处理此问题。

blocks.html_en: The part that makes this work is Transmission's downloadDirPermissions set to 2775. The 2 indicates that the setgid bit is on, which means that if /srv/linux-isos is owned by the media group, every file and directory created inside it will inherit the media group rather than the creator's primary group.

sentences.en: The part that makes this work is Transmission's downloadDirPermissions set to 2775.
sentences.zh: 使这项工作有效的部分是 Transmission 的 downloadDirPermissions 设置为 2775。

sentences.en: The 2 indicates that the setgid bit is on, which means that if /srv/linux-isos is owned by the media group, every file and directory created inside it will inherit the media group rather than the creator's primary group.
sentences.zh: 2 表示 setgid 位已打开，这意味着如果 /srv/linux-isos 归媒体组所有，则在其中创建的每个文件和目录都将继承媒体组而不是创建者的主要组。

blocks.html_en: Monitoring

sentences.en: Monitoring
sentences.zh: 监控

blocks.html_en: This is one part I haven't fully figured out yet. The only precaution I have taken is to regularly run btrfs scrubs on the pool to connect any errors and decide whether to take a closer look at the S.M.A.R.T stats based on the number of errors on each drive.

sentences.en: This is one part I haven't fully figured out yet.
sentences.zh: 这是我还没有完全弄清楚的一部分。

sentences.en: The only precaution I have taken is to regularly run btrfs scrubs on the pool to connect any errors and decide whether to take a closer look at the S.M.A.R.T stats based on the number of errors on each drive.
sentences.zh: 我采取的唯一预防措施是定期在池上运行 btrfs 清理以连接任何错误，并根据每个驱动器上的错误数量决定是否仔细查看 S.M.A.R.T 统计信息。

I know next to nothing about Grafana so it all looks a bit daunting and overkill to me, but it might be a good learning experience.

blocks.html_en: Feeding the pool

sentences.en: Feeding the pool

blocks.html_en: Let's get back to the spinning rust; we were at two disks in the pool. I wanted to add a 1TB drive to the pool while also copying its contents to it. The disk was already partitioned exactly in half and most of the data was in its back half, so I formatted the front half as btrfs, added it to the pool, copied the back half to the pool, and then extended the btrfs partition to fill the disk. I'm not sure how effective this was in terms of speed, but it saved me a btrfs balance.

content_html_zh:
几天前的晚上，我和我的伙伴想要安装这个特定的 Linux ISO。 Red Letter Systems 的人对此评价很好，在boundingboxd 上也有很好的评价，我的搭档也喜欢安装预告片。

我在手机上打开 Tremotesf 以检查 Pi 上是否已安装它，但 torrent 列表无法加载。 SMB 安装无法安装。 我也无法通过 SSH 连接到它。

我并不急于立即开始调试会话来破坏这个夜晚，我将其归结为最近对 NixOS 配置所做的更改的一个简单问题，并决定第二天查看它。

第二天

我将 Pi 连接到衣柜里的专用调试显示器，敲响指关节，说“调试时间到了”之类的话，然后重新启动了砖块。

当前的 NixOS 一代开始启动、加载内核并...... 挂在那里。 没有更多的输出。 上一代也做了同样的事情。 我把microSD卡拿出来进一步检查。

第一个明显的步骤是对其进行 fsck；几天前，整栋大楼神秘地断电了，也许就在写入包含一些对启动过程很重要的文件的块之一时？ 无论如何，这是值得一试的。 fsck.ext4 报告并修复了许多错误。 再次运行它报告 FS 是干净的，但是弹出然后将卡重新插入会出现更多错误......

长话短说，这张卡很可能已经死了，而停电只是造成了最后的致命一击。 按F表示敬意。

盘点库存

这个 Raspberry Pi 4B 已经运行了很多年，几乎 24/7，所以这并不令人意外。 我的理解是，SD 卡在出现故障之前可以承受相对较少的写入周期。 令人惊讶的是，考虑到我对待它的态度有多糟糕，我的机器竟然运行了这么长时间，没有出现任何问题。 microSD 作为根 FS 挂载，我几乎没有采取任何预防措施来最大程度地减少写入。

综合考虑，我并没有损失太多。 重要的东西大多存在于外部 HDD 中，而配置几乎完全存在于我的 NixOS 配置中。 microSD 卡上唯一存在且我没有备份的东西是 .torrent 文件以及 Navidrome、Jellyfin 和 slskd 的缓存，所有这些我都可以轻松地重新生成。 Immich 的数据安全地备份在外部驱动器上，并且我定期进行备份，因此没有丢失任何内容。

我以前从来没有遇到过像这样的“主要”驱动器消失的情况；我见过的最糟糕的是一些失效的闪存驱动器，其中没有包含任何重要的内容。 自从我十几岁的时候第一次尝试安装 Linux 时不小心损坏了我的 MacBook 上的主 OS X 分区以来，对驱动器失效和数据丢失的恐惧就一直存在于我的脑海中，但现在情况比以往任何时候都更糟，所以我想采取强有力的预防措施。

配置新设置

我开始重建我的家庭服务器，考虑到以下几点：

  • 尽量减少对 microSD 的写入。
  • 外部硬盘驱动器上存储的数据有一些冗余。
  • 备份所有重要的东西。

Swap、/tmp 和其他易失性数据

即使我的 Pi 上有 8GB RAM，交换功能也很有用，但如果我希望它能长久使用，我认为交换到 microSD 并不是一个好主意，所以我可以交换到外部 HDD（非常慢）或使用 zram。

zram，以前称为 compcache，是一个 Linux 内核模块，用于在 RAM 中创建压缩块设备，即具有即时磁盘压缩功能的 RAM 磁盘。

zram 的常见用途是 swap 或 /tmp。 我选择为交换启用 zram，但不为 /tmp 启用，我将其保留为普通 RAM 磁盘；我不是 100% 确定这两个功能如何交互，但我想我应该让内核弄清楚如何以及何时将 /tmp 中的冷页交换到压缩交换，而不是为其保留单独的块。

{
  # Enable in-memory compressed swap device
  zramSwap.enable = true;

  # Use a RAM disk for /tmp
  boot.tmp.useTmpfs = true;
}

我还选择将日志记录到内存而不是磁盘。 我需要调查是否可以记录到内存，并且只是偶尔刷新到磁盘（在这种情况下，我会将 /var/log 安装到 HDD 上的子卷），但我还没有弄清楚这部分。

{
  # Store journal logs in memory to /run/log/journal
  services.journald.storage = "volatile";
}

microSD 卡仍然安装在 / 上，但我禁用了 atime，因为每当访问文件时它都会导致写入，我认为它不是很有用。

{
  fileSystems."/" = {
    device = "/dev/disk/by-label/takodachi";
    fsType = "ext4";
    options = [ "noatime" ];
  };
}

外置硬盘

如果您生活在 2026 年，您可能知道现在不是购买任何新驱动器（或 RAM、GPU，或任何可用于构建数据中心并满足痴迷于 AI 精神病的高管的口袋的东西）的好时机，最好利用您所拥有的东西来发挥斗志和创造力。

我有大量各种品牌、尺寸和寿命的驱动器。 我主要将它们视为我想保留的旧数据的冷存储；它们相当于数字垃圾抽屉。 我一直想为了更高的目的而牺牲它们，但直到现在才找到一个。 我从一堆旧的 2.5 英寸硬盘中取出了两个。

第一个是包含 Windows 文件系统的 500GB 驱动器。 这用在我伙伴的笔记本电脑上；来自另一个商务机器时代的 3KG 庞然大物，更多地意味着它是一台可以轻松移动到另一张桌子并偶尔带回家的台式机，而不是真正的便携式机器。 首先，我将 HDD 换成了 SSD，作为对即将死去的笔记本电脑的怜悯之举；最终，我为我的合作伙伴安装了一台运行 NixOS 的 Surface Pro 5，他们好心地给了我旧笔记本电脑和硬盘来使用。

第二个是 320GB 驱动器，似乎包含... 家庭助理安装？ 我不知道那是怎么结束的。 不管怎样，这是一个带有苹果标志的日立硬盘，所以它曾经放在我的 2006 年白色塑料 MacBook 或类似年份的 mac mini 中。

我将这两条历史记录连接到一个扩展坞，并创建了一个具有 raid1 复制的 btrfs 池。 我恰当地将它命名为“垃圾”。

sudo mkfs.btrfs --data raid1 --metadata raid1 --label ponkotsu /dev/sdX /dev/sdY

我选择了 btrfs，因为这是我所知道的（它在我的台式机和笔记本电脑上运行），我喜欢子卷（稍后会详细介绍），而且我喜欢它的池的灵活性。 您不必事先规划池的拓扑，可以随时将驱动器添加到池中，并且它们的大小不必相同。

子卷

我的计划是每个服务都有一个 btrfs 子卷，并且为了以声明方式执行此操作，我创建了一个 autosubvol 模块。 配置如下：

{
  services.autosubvol = {
    enable = true;
    disks.ponkotsu = {
      device = "/dev/disk/by-uuid/<pool uuid>";
      subvolumes.immich = {
        mountPoint = "/var/lib/immich";
        mountOptions = [ "noatime" ];
        requiredBy = [ "immich-server.service" ];
      };
    };
  };
}

该模块创建一些 systemd 单元：

  • /run/btrfs-roots/<name> 处的每个磁盘有一个 .mount 单元。
  • 一个 autosubvol-ensure-<disk>-<subvolume>.service oneshot 单元，其中包含一个脚本，用于检查子卷是否存在，如果不存在，则创建它。 它配置为 RemainAfterExit=true，以便子卷的 .mount 单元可以依赖它。
  • 每个子卷一个 .mount 单元，安装到指定的安装点。 它取决于 autosubvol-ensure .service 并根据 requiredBy 的单位设置 Before= 和RequiredBy=。

我认为这使得每个服务模块看起来都很干净。

{
  config,
  ...
}:
{
  services.immich = {
    enable = true;
    # ...
  };

  services.autosubvol.disks.ponkotsu.subvolumes.immich = {
    mountPoint = config.services.immich.mediaLocation;
    mountOptions = [ "noatime" ];
    requiredBy = [ "immich-server.service" ];
  };
}

为了更好地衡量，我还为 /var/cache 创建了一个子卷。

{
  fileSystems."/var/cache" = {
    device = "/dev/disk/by-uuid/...";
    fsType = "btrfs";
    options = [
      "subvol=@cache"
      "noatime"
      "nofail"
    ];
  };
}

备份

当我设置 Immich 时，我还创建了一个 Nix 模块，充当 sops-nix 和 Restic nixpkgs 模块之间的桥梁。 实现只有几行代码，在我的 Nix 配置中如下所示：

{
  internal.backups.restic.immich = {
    paths = [ "/var/lib/immich" ];
    exclude = [
      "/var/lib/immich/encoded-video"
      "/var/lib/immich/thumbs"
    ];
    pruneOpts = [
      "--keep-daily 7"
      "--keep-weekly 4"
      "--keep-monthly 3"
    ];
  };
}

由于每个服务都位于其自己的子卷上，我可能可以使用快照和 btrfs 发送一个完全专用于备份的磁盘来设置本地备份系统，我大部分时间都会保持关闭状态，但由于现在我没有那么多磁盘，而且获取新磁盘变得越来越困难，所以我将把它留到更好的时候。 必须使用 S3 存储桶和复制。

组和权限

我希望多个服务能够访问我的 Linux ISO：Transmission、Jellyfin、Navidrome 以及其他一些服务。 我通过创建一个媒体组并将其作为额外组分配给所有需要读取或写入这些文件的应用程序来处理此问题。

{
  users.groups.media = {};
  users.users.steenuil.extraGroups = [ "media" ];
  users.users.jellyfin.extraGroups = [ "media" ];

  services.transmission = {
    group = "media";
    downloadDirPermissions = "2775";
    settings.umask = "002";
  };
}

使这项工作有效的部分是 Transmission 的 downloadDirPermissions 设置为 2775。 2 表示 setgid 位已打开，这意味着如果 /srv/linux-isos 归媒体组所有，则在其中创建的每个文件和目录都将继承媒体组而不是创建者的主要组。

监控

这是我还没有完全弄清楚的一部分。 我采取的唯一预防措施是定期在池上运行 btrfs 清理以连接任何错误，并根据每个驱动器上的错误数量决定是否仔细查看 S.M.A.R.T 统计信息。

我想设置 smartd 并可能将其连接到像 Scrutiny 这样的漂亮仪表板，但由于我正在运行许多其他服务并且我不介意对它们进行更多统计，也许设置 Prometheus 然后弄清楚如何在漂亮的 Grafana 仪表板上显示所有内容会更有用？ 我对 Grafana 几乎一无所知，所以这对我来说看起来有点令人畏惧和过大，但这可能是一次很好的学习经历。

给泳池喂食

让我们回到旋转的铁锈；我们在池中的两个磁盘上。 我想向池中添加一个 1TB 驱动器，同时将其内容复制到其中。 磁盘已经被精确地划分为两半，大部分数据都在后半部分，因此我将前半部分格式化为 btrfs，将其添加到池中，将后半部分复制到池中，然后扩展 btrfs 分区以填充磁盘。 我不确定这在速度方面有多有效，但它为我节省了 btrfs 平衡。

为了节省 Pi 的一些麻烦，我在桌面上设置了 Immich 并从备份中恢复，并且由于我避免在前面的步骤中移动缩略图，所以我在 webp 中重新生成了所有缩略图。

这是池的当前状态：一个 1TB 磁盘、一个 500GB 和一个 320GB 磁盘； raid1 用于数据和元数据。 我计划向其中添加另一个旧的 500GB 磁盘，并且我可能会保持这种状态，直到驱动器价格下降或其中一个驱动器损坏为止。

我还有一个仍然使用 ext4 格式化的 10TB 磁盘，里面装满了我无法合理添加到池中的 Linux ISO，所以我只会备份我认为重要的任何内容，然后一直使用它，直到它耗尽为止。 #yolo

新的 microSD

由于我对 microSD 卡的压力要小得多，所以我选择了 64GB，而不是之前使用的 128GB。 我寻找“高耐用性”卡，因为它们在放弃幽灵之前应该有更多的写入周期。

但我并不完全确定我最终得到了什么。 这整个磨难发生在我写这篇文章的假期前几天，所以我订购了一张卡片，它将在周六到达：我离开的前一天。 周六早上到了，包裹还没发货，我只好取消订单，赶紧下车，步行到最近的家电商店。

还不错。 我愉快地散步了，拍了一些亚洲甲虫的照片，这些甲虫正在入侵南欧，留下了被吃掉一半的叶子的痕迹。

商店里有空调。 我看了一下最新的噱头手机。 我带着一张号称高耐用性的64GB microSD卡走出去，甚至还有一个打折的UPS。

部署

当您刚开始时，NixOS 是一个令人生畏的项目。 互联网上散布着太多的信息，无法完全掌握。 不过你已经习惯了。 你的大脑负责翻译。

您可以通过下载官方 NixOS 映像并从空系统启动来在 Pi 上引导 NixOS 安装，或者您可以在内心深处知道您所需要的只是这个模块：

{
  modulesPath,
  ...
}:
{
  imports = [
    "${modulesPath}/installer/sd-card/sd-image-aarch64.nix"
  ];

  sdImage.rootVolumeLabel = "takodachi";
}

为了能够施展这个咒语：

nix build .#nixosConfigurations.takodachi.config.system.build.sdImage

这将构建一个 .img 文件，您可以将其添加到 microSD 卡以使系统启动并运行，无需任何中间步骤。

整理完毕

在对 microSD 卡进行成像后，我所要做的就是将所有东西连接到新的 UPS 并将其插入，几秒钟后我就可以通过 SSH 连接到 Pi 并开始解决新配置的一些小问题。

我去度假的时间有点晚了，但 Navidrome 已启动并运行，并且能够提供音乐供开车时聆听。
